puzzle0.title: IDENTITY RECONSTRUCTION
puzzle0.description: Rebuild the erased seventh crew member from recovered fragments. Wrong answers destabilize the archive.

event6.text: Access spine is tighter than the schematic promised.

event8.text: I know. Shocking. A technical diagram lied to us.

event10.text: Smells like hot plastic, old dust, and something I am officially not naming until I have to.

event13.text: Noah said you already saw his name.

event15.text: If that's true, it's buried in records we've already touched.

event18.text: And before we start pretending yesterday didn't happen—

event20.text: if you asked me what I did back there, I remember.

event24.text: That is not the same thing as being fine with you.

event26.text: The memory index is three junctions ahead.

event7.text: I'm stepping over them, not on them.

backgroundEvent8.text: The wall just clicked. I hate walls with timing.

event10.text: There's a maintenance drone in the wall.

event18.text: I can check it, or keep moving before my courage files a complaint.

event3.text: Low ceiling. Narrow walls. Deeply rude to knees.

event7.text: If I get stuck, delete this from any heroic version of the transcript.

backgroundEvent8.text: Cable brushed my neck. I handled that with dignity. You cannot prove otherwise.

event10.text: There are old cable tags in here.

event14.text: Rivera hated handwriting on system labels.

choice1.text: IGNORE IT AND REACH THE INDEX.

event5.text: Someone forced it open from the outside.

event7.text: There's a route packet tucked under the diagnostic core.

event9.text: That does not belong in a maintenance drone.

event13.text: Nothing today belongs where it belongs.

event15.text: I need both hands to pull it without shredding the buffer.

event4.title: DRONE ROUTE CACHE
event4.content:
ROUTE CACHE / PARTIAL
DESTINATION: PERSONNEL MEMORY INDEX
RECENT QUERY FRAGMENT: ...VOSS...
OPERATOR: UNKNOWN
CACHE STATUS: DAMAGED
event4.caption: Recovered route packet from damaged maintenance drone.
event4.message: [DRONE ROUTE CACHE ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event7.text: Spliced twice.

event9.text: Someone rerouted a person like a circuit.

event14.text: I hate that it made sense.

event16.text: I'm peeling the tag loose. Carefully.

event6.title: HANDWRITTEN CABLE TAG
event6.content:
HANDWRITTEN LABEL RECOVERED
TEXT: VOSS / MEMORY INDEX BYPASS
INK AGE: PRE-BLACKOUT
AUTHORIZED LABEL FORMAT: INVALID
event6.caption: A handwritten cable tag found inside the service crawl.
event6.message: [HANDWRITTEN CABLE TAG ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event5.text: The station keeps offering little reasons to stop.

event7.text: Could be clues.

event14.text: If that turns out stupid, please remember I looked determined.

event5.text: Somebody wanted this shut even if the station woke up.

event7.text: Handle's cold through the glove.

event11.text: Or bad news pretending to be a temperature.

event13.text: I can force it, or scan the lock first.

event7.text: That makes it an operational decision, apparently.

event5.text: Tamper loop under the manual latch.

event7.text: If I forced this, the local cache would've wiped itself.

event9.text: Which is exactly the sort of petty behavior I expect from this place.

event17.text: Like a person who absolutely slept this week.

event7.text: One is blinking.

event11.text: More like it's trying to get attention without looking desperate.

event4.title: CORRUPTED MANIFEST FRAGMENT
event4.content:
CREW MEMBER #07
STATUS: ERASED
SURNAME: VOSS
MEMORY INDEX: DAMAGED
SOURCE CONFLICT: OFFICIAL MANIFEST DENIES ENTRY
event4.caption: Partially recovered crew record from the personnel memory index.
event4.message: [CORRUPTED MANIFEST FRAGMENT ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event16.text: I don't remember anyone named Voss on ECHO.

event19.text: I don't like how easy that was to say.

backgroundEvent20.text: YOU REMEMBER THE SHAPE OF THE HOLE.

event7.text: I have earned the criticism.

event9.text: But I know what that name can do in this system.

event11.text: Names are not labels in ECHO's identity layer.

event17.text: Some wake things that were shut down for a reason.

event19.text: Maya, take your hand off the core.

event7.text: Denied. Sealed. Filed under Omega clearance conflict.

event9.text: That is institutional language for: do not ask while anyone important is listening.

event14.text: I do not remember the answer.

event16.text: That fact is becoming less comforting by the minute.

event3.text: Hale can panic in complete sentences.

event5.text: I am going to make the dead computer talk.

event7.text: Also, Ethan, if this kills me, I am haunting your punctuation.

event11.text: That means he either hated the joke or is worried.

event0.title: MAINTENANCE LOG
event0.content:
Relay inspection completed.
Signal bleed detected on emergency carrier.
Manual reset recommended.

Signed: N. Voss
event0.caption: Recovered relay maintenance entry with partial signature.

event6.text: Maintenance log gives us N. Voss.

event8.text: Noah is starting to look less like a ghost story.

event10.text: More like a person someone removed very carefully.

event13.text: And if you're wondering whether I am okay with that sentence—

choice1.text: TELL MAYA TO STEP BACK FIRST.

event7.text: Fighting.

event11.text: It keeps redirecting the query into an empty crew slot.

event17.text: No, I don't have a better verb.

event3.text: I hate that I needed someone else to say that.

event7.text: Not dramatically.

event9.text: Just enough to make small buttons feel personally insulting.

event3.text: I have to keep the core awake manually.

event5.text: If I let go, it drops the fragment and we start over.

event7.text: So I'm standing here with one hand on a hot memory core.

event0.text: Maya is holding the memory core open. No new choices until she returns.

event3.text: My glove is warm from the core housing.

event7.text: Close enough to make me polite.

event9.text: We have the shape of him now.

event4.title: IDENTITY FRAGMENT
event4.content:
CREW MEMBER #07
SURNAME: VOSS
INITIAL: N.
ROLE CLASS: COMMUNICATIONS
MEMORY INDEX STATE: SUPPRESSED
TRANSFER STATE: CONTRADICTORY
event4.caption: Recovered identity fragment assembled from memory index and maintenance records.

event16.text: If we enter the name, the system may restore the profile.

event18.text: Or it may let something answer to it.

event1.text: Give it exactly what the records support.

event7.text: The system will accept confidence it has not earned.

event9.text: That is how bad archives become worse gods.

event14.text: If you do this, do it cleanly.

event7.text: No improvising.

event15.text: We are not inventing a person.

event5.text: Role class: communications. The recovered answer is Noah Voss.

event7.text: I wish saying that did not feel like turning a handle from the wrong side of a door.

event1.text: Okay. I will walk you through it.

event5.text: Not just Voss. Not just Noah if the panel gets picky.

event7.text: And if the archive screams, we pretend that is normal and keep our hands steady.

event1.title: NOAH VOSS
event1.content:
NOAH VOSS
ROLE: COMMUNICATIONS SPECIALIST
CLEARANCE LEVEL: OMEGA
STATUS: UNKNOWN
TRANSFER STATUS: DENIED
MEMORY INDEX: SUPPRESSED
event1.caption: Reconstructed personnel profile from fragmented archive records.
event1.message: [NOAH VOSS PROFILE ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event4.text: There is a second after the profile opens where nobody says anything.

event8.text: Because a deleted man just got his name back.

event10.text: I don't know whether to be relieved or furious.

event7.text: The first person erased from ECHO was the one whose job was to make sure people could speak.

event0.title: TRANSFER RECORD: NOAH VOSS
event0.content:
TRANSFER REQUEST: NOAH VOSS
ROLE: COMMUNICATIONS SPECIALIST
STATUS: DENIED
DATE: 21 DAYS BEFORE LAUNCH
REASON: OMEGA CLEARANCE CONFLICT
FINAL NOTE: DO NOT ASSIGN TO ECHO
event0.caption: Recovered transfer record showing Noah Voss was officially denied station assignment.
event0.message: [NOAH VOSS TRANSFER RECORD ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event7.text: Then why was his name inside our station?

event9.text: Why was his handwriting in our walls?

event11.text: Why does the archive flinch when we say him out loud?

event8.text: Not where they let you remember me.

event11.text: I was the voice behind the static.

event17.text: The one Hale never told you about.

event3.text: Drone tried to wipe the packet when I touched the core.

event7.text: Nothing panics unless it has something to hide.

event14.text: Voss was not just in a manifest layer.

event7.text: Not printed.

event12.text: I hate how personal this is starting to feel.

event7.text: No cable tag.

event10.text: Just a sealed hatch and the feeling that I skipped something that wanted to be found.

event4.text: Also sparked directly into my sleeve.

event9.text: My sleeve has filed a disagreement.

event11.text: And my shoulder is going to remember your leadership style.

event5.text: That is now my favorite kind of door.

event7.text: I am choosing to believe caution is not always cowardice.
